# RTK Prefix Enforcement

RTK is a CLI proxy that filters and compresses command output, saving 60-90% tokens.
All shell commands eligible for RTK must be written with the `rtk` prefix in
documentation, skills, and agent files so that agents learn and replicate the correct
pattern.

## Eligible commands

`git`, `pytest`, `ruff`, `docker`, `uv`, `pip`, `mypy`, `prek`

## How to comply

Prefix every eligible command in fenced shell blocks:

```bash
rtk git status
rtk pytest tests/
rtk uv sync
```

## Suppressing a block

Place `<!-- rtk:skip -->` on the line immediately before a fenced code block to exempt
the entire block from the check. Use this only when the commands are illustrative
examples of output, not instructions to run.

## Vendor skills (speckit-*)

Vendor skills are generated by the `specify` CLI and are normally not edited.
For RTK compliance, edit them directly to add the `rtk` prefix. Do NOT use
`<!-- rtk:skip -->` suppression in vendor skill files. Adding the prefix is a
non-breaking, semantically correct change that survives `specify upgrade` review.
